news.title: Zlatan, Thierry Henry On Spain's World Cup Victory: 'Football Won Today'
news.lede: Following Spain's 1-0 victory over Argentina in the World Cup final, the FOX Sports duo of Zlatan Ibrahimović and Thierry Henry praised Spain's tactical execution and defensive performance, which limited Argentina's offensive output throughout the game. Ibrahimović emphasized that Spain's victory was a positive result for the sport due to the commitment to the team's own style of play. He noted that teams attempting to play soccer with finesse often face criticism if they fail to win, but Spain proved sticking to its approach helped secure a championship. "Football won today," Ibrahimović said.

news.title: President Donald Trump presents World Cup trophy to Spain
news.lede: President Donald Trump presented the World Cup trophy to Spain on Sunday, July 19 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford. Trump and FIFA President Gianni Infantino heard boos as they walked on the field for the postgame ceremony. Spain won its second World Cup on an extra time goal by Ferran Torres. The country won its first World Cup in 2010 also on an extra time goal to beat the Netherlands, 1-0.

news.title: Spain win the World Cup after Ferran Torres dethrones 10-man Argentina in extra time
news.lede: Enzo Fernández sent off for two bookings in normal time. Victory secures second men’s World Cup title for La Roja. Spain finally broke the dogged resistance of 10-man Argentina in extra time to win the men’s World Cup for the second time as a drab final ended in a dramatic fashion. The Barcelona winger Ferran Torres scored the only goal of a disjointed game in the 106th minute, with Argentina’s cynical approach summed up by a mass confrontation sparked by Leandro Paredes pushing Gavi and Eric García to the ground after the final whistle, which led to the Boca Juniors midfield player following Enzo Fernández in being shown a red card.

news.title: Spain 1-0 Argentina (aet): 2026 World Cup final player ratings
news.lede: Rodri looks back to his imperious best in Spain’s midfield while Enzo Fernández has day to forget for Argentina. Unai Simón Was alert to clear danger after Cubarsí’s early mistake. Otherwise could have brought his slippers. Pedro Porro Doesn’t look like a player who was in a relegation battle a few weeks ago.

news.title: BBC keep it serious while ITV bring the fun despite ‘awful’ half-time show | Will Unwin
news.lede: BBC scored a late goal in head-to-head with ITV over the World Cup by being in the stadium for the final. “I thought it was crap,” Wayne Rooney surmised on BBC after enduring the half-time show. Amid a tense final, the interval was always going to be prominent, theoretically bringing light to the shade. Why the two UK broadcasters felt the need to transmit the karaoke session in full we will never know but at least the pundits struggled through it, too.

news.title: World Cup schedule, results: Spain claims its second title with extra-time triumph over defending champion Argentina
news.lede: Spain has won its second World Cup championship with a grueling extra-time 1-0 victory over Argentina. The defending European champions peppered Argentine goal keeper Emiliano Martínez with 20 shots throughout the 120-plus minutes while La Albiceleste did not register a shot of their own until extra time. Martínez would break the final saves record with his ninth stop of the match late in regulation. Spain forward Ferran Torres netted the winner in the 106th minute, scoring off a deflection in the Argentina box.

news.title: Spain beats Argentina 1-0 to capture second World Cup title
news.lede: Ferran Torres scored in the 106th minute to lift Spain to their second World Cup title with a 1-0 win in extra time over gutsy, 10-man Argentina in Sunday's final. Torres' deserved winner came 16 years and eight days after Andres Iniesta also scored in extra time to lift the Spaniards to their first World Championship with a 1-0 victory over the Netherlands. It came on La Roja's 20th shot on the contest against a defending champion Argentina that failed to manage any to that point and struggled to wrest possession away from their superior Iberian opponents. That was true at even strength and became more difficult when the Albiceleste's numbers were reduced when Enzo Fernandez's late challenge of Pau Cubarsi left referee Slavko Vincic no choice but to produce a second caution just before the end of normal time.

news.title: Spain’s Rodri wins World Cup Golden Ball as Pau Cubarsi, Unai Simon also collect awards
news.lede: France's Kylian Mbappe won the Golden Boot award with 10 goals.
